channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: A major space sector trade group is getting a brand refresh
item.description: The trade association formerly known as the Commercial Spaceflight Federation is getting a new name, a concise mandate, and a way to support the politicians who support space.
item.dc:creator: Jacqueline Feldsher, Fast Company

item.title: Founder of failed crypto lending platform Celsius Network pleads guilty to fraud charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The founder and former CEO of the failed cryptocurrency lending platform Celsius Network could face decades in prison after pleading guilty Tuesday to federal fraud charges, admitting that he misled customers about the business.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China responds to U.S chip sanctions with a ban on these key materials
item.description: China announced Tuesday it is banning exports to the United States of gallium, germanium, antimony, and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications, as a general principle, lashing back at U.S. limits on semiconductor-related exports.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Consumer watchdog wants FTC to investigate Oz for influencer marketing violations
item.description: A consumer advocacy group wants the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to open an investigation into whether Dr. Mehmet Oz violated influencer marketing rules by promoting products from a wellness and supplement company across social media without appropriate disclosures.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, The Hill

item.title: Arista adds intelligent switch stacking, management for campus networks
item.description: Arista Networks is adding new features to its campus networking portfolio to help enterprise customers more easily group and manage switches.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Elon Musk's Neuralink expands offices in Austin area
item.description: Musk's brain-computer interface company, Neuralink, will expand in the area with more space and job openings.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Al-Shaikh, NewsNation

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez first person to hit a million followers on Bluesky
item.description: Other than Bluesky’s own account, Ocasio-Cortez’s follower landmark is a first for a user, the platform told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Federal Executives Share Success Stories and Vision for the Future
item.description: Federal government technology executives share IT success stories, best practices, and future vision in a webinar discussion
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Bay Area native’s online talk show interviews AI chatbots
item.description: Not knowing where these conversations will go, the show "allows us to enjoy the technology while also enjoying our humanity.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Bidwill, The Mercury News

item.title: TikTok defends handling of Romania election content in grilling by EU lawmakers
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — TikTok took down several networks that tried to meddle in Romania's elections, executives said Tuesday as they defended the company's election integrity measures to European Union lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN and STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jury selection begins in 2019 Santa Cruz tech entrepreneur kidnapping, homicide
item.description: Tech and cannabis entrepreneur Tushar Atre was kidnapped from his Pleasure Point home in the middle of the night in October 2019.
item.dc:creator: Jessica York, The Mercury News

item.title: China bans exports to US of gallium, germanium, antimony in response to chip sanctions
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China announced Tuesday it is banning exports to the United States of gallium, germanium, antimony and other key high-tech materials with potential military applications, as a general principle, lashing back at U.S. limits on semiconductor-related exports.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The transformation of shared services
item.description: Over the past several decades, organizations in both the private and public sectors have turned to shared services. Sometimes, the goal was reducing operational costs or improving efficiency. Other times, it was more about consolidating functions and improving service.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Robotic shorts give your legs a boost to walk farther and tire less
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how WalkON enhances walking and jogging with sensors and artificial tendons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Consumer watchdog proposes crackdown on personal data sales
item.description: The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) on Tuesday rolled out a new proposal aimed at cracking down on data brokers selling Americans' sensitive information.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, The Hill

item.title: How AI agents will transform the future of work
item.description: At first, robotic process automation coupled with low-code platforms and orchestration tools propelled many organizations to increase productivity and scale business operations. Virtual agents and chatbots then took automation one step further by enabling a conversational experience.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AWS tries to lure users to its cloud via storage ease of use
item.description: At its re:Invent 2024 conference this week, AWS rolled out five new storage tools and services with the goal of making its offerings easier to work with, if not cheaper or better. With the incremental differences in the major enterprise cloud environments today, that may be enough.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Cyber Monday shoppers expected to set a record on the year's biggest day for online shopping
item.description: Consumers in the United States are scouring the internet for online deals as they look to take advantage of the post-Thanksgiving shopping marathon with Cyber Monday.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI chatbots can make your Cyber Monday shopping a breeze, but they’re not foolproof
item.description: Tired of thinking about what gifts to get everyone this year? Artificial intelligence chatbots might help, but don’t expect them to do all the work or always give you the right answers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: FTC opens Microsoft antitrust investigation that Trump administration must carry on or drop
item.description: Antitrust enforcers with the Federal Trade Commission have opened a wide-ranging investigation into Microsoft's business practices, starting a big legal project that an incoming Trump administration must take up or abandon.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Why Sasha Luccioni wants to create Energy Star ratings for AI
item.description: Sasha Luccioni was experiencing something of a quarter-life crisis. It was early 2018, and Luccioni was working as an AI researcher at Morgan Stanley, where she spent her days thinking about how to use the rapidly advancing technology to head off financial risks.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: Bipartisan bill would study AI's impact on banking, housing
item.description: A new bill with bipartisan support would commission studies on the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in the banking and housing sectors, where lawmakers have been sounding an alarm about algorithmic price fixing and commercial misconduct enabled by new technologies.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, The Hill

item.title: How AI is accelerating—and devaluing—book publishing
item.description: AI has the power to pump out words in record speed. And already, that’s substantially inflating the book publishing market. Lovers of LLMs are pushing into the traditional publishing system, building their own book-publishing entities.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Panasonic revives its late founder as AI
item.description: Japanese electronics company Panasonic has created an AI version of its founder, Kōnosuke Matsushita, who died in 1989, The Japan Times reports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger retires; 2 named as interim co-CEOs
item.description: The departure of Gelsinger underscores the turmoil at Intel, which has been eclipsed by rival Nvidia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Intel CEO steps down as chipmaker falters, lays off staff
item.description: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger has stepped down after a three-year stint leading the company, the chipmaker announced Monday. Two senior executives, David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus, will take over as interim co-CEOs amid the search for a new chief executive.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Data broker blunder: More than 600,000 sensitive files exposed in data services leak
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how 600,000-plus records, including checks and property data, were left exposed online by an information service provider.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Robots perform like human surgeons by just watching videos
item.description: University scientists have trained a robotic surgical system to do work with the skill of human doctors, possibly transforming the future of surgeries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
